title: Edge Function DB-error logs omit roomId/playerId context on 9 of 10 functions
evidence: Every DB-error catch across these functions follows the same shape, e.g. join_room/index.ts:66 `console.error('join_room: fetch error', fetchError)` and restart_game/index.ts:90 `console.error('restart_game: fetch error', fetchError)` — the request's roomId/playerId (already in scope as `roomId`/`playerId`/`room.id`) is never included, only the raw Postgrest error object. submit_action/index.ts:121 `console.error('submit_action: fetch error', fetchError)` has the same gap even though the same file's rpc-error/turn-gate paths (lines 39, 165) already learned this lesson via #67/#79 (obs-002/003/006), which added `{ bucket, ip }` / `{ roomId, storedActiveIndex, actingId }` context specifically so an aggregated multi-tenant log stream is traceable back to one room.
impact: Supabase Edge Function logs interleave concurrent invocations across every active room with no per-request correlation id. A `join_room: update error {...}` or `restart_game: claim error {...}` line in the aggregated stream cannot be tied to which room/player triggered it without cross-referencing timestamps, defeating exactly the diagnosability the prior obs pass established for the 3 signals it did fix. The gap is inconsistent, not absolute: the fix pattern already exists in the same files (submit_action:34,95,165) and just wasn't applied to the surrounding fetch/update/insert error sites.
recommendation: Extend the { roomId, playerId } (or room.id) context object already used in the #67/#79 signals to the remaining console.error call sites in each function — no new logging infra required, just apply the existing convention consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Each console.error/warn on a DB error path in the 10 Edge Functions includes the roomId and/or playerId already in scope at that call site.
